finding_results.notes: Replaced both instances of the 'asked/asks if I'm eating well' cadence. Line 113 now reads 'She wanted to know how the food is' — a different verb, different object, different syntactic shape from the style-bible example, while preserving Marcus's casual register and the banal-domestic horror of the beat. Line 137 now reads 'his ex-wife asking about the food' — compressed indirect reference that does not repeat the dialogue phrasing, landing the same gap between mundane question and death-scene reality without echoing either the style-bible calibration line or Marcus's in-scene delivery. The two instances now use distinct phrasing from each other and from the style-bible example.

summary: Single finding addressed. The ex-wife relay-message beat at lines 113 and 137 was rewritten to eliminate the 'asked/asks if I'm eating well' cadence that echoed the style-bible calibration example. Both passages now land the same emotional function — mundane domestic concern arriving in a death scenario — through genuinely different phrasing. Marcus's voice (casual, high-contraction, trailing off on personal subjects) is preserved. No collateral changes to surrounding text, chapter structure, heading contract, or continuity obligations.
